steps.title: Set the sides equal and simplify
steps.content:
$7ax - 9a = 7ax - 14a - 35$
Subtract $7ax$ from both sides: $-9a = -14a - 35$
Add $14a$ to both sides: $5a = -35$
Divide by 5: $a = -7$

steps.title: Verify: does a = -7 give infinitely many solutions?
steps.content:
Substitute $a = -7$:
Left: $7(-7)x - 9(-7) = -49x + 63$
Right: $7(-7)x - 14(-7) - 35 = -49x + 98 - 35 = -49x + 63$
Left = Right for all $x$, confirming infinitely many solutions.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '0 only': Substituting $a = 0$ gives $0 = -35$, a contradiction. ✗
  • Choice B — '-7 only': Derived algebraically above; $a = -7$ is the unique value making the equation an identity. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Any real number': Most values of $a$ yield a false constant equation (no solution) or exactly one solution, not all reals. ✗
  • Choice D — 'No real number': We found $a = -7$ works, so this is false. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
